headline: SLOP WATCH ISSUED ACROSS THE SEARCH RESULTS BASIN AS FABRICATED-HERITAGE FRONT PRODUCES 'RARE HISTORICAL PHOTOS' OF EVENTS THAT LEFT NO NEGATIVE
advisory: A slow-moving system of synthetic nostalgia has settled over image-heavy channels, generating sepia-toned 'archival' photographs of moments that were never photographed and, in several cases, never occurred. Residents are advised that a caption reading 'colorized, 1911' is not a chain of custody. The Service reminds the public that genuine antique photographs contain fewer than the customary number of fingers only by accident, not by manufacture. Zoom before you share.

regional_outlook.conditions: Heavy fabricated-heritage accumulation, with 'unearthed' portraits drifting to the top of image results. Reverse-image visibility near zero.
Warm and saturated, with dense boards of 'vintage everyday life' that never existed pooling in the low-lying feeds. Repinning expected to intensify overnight.
Scattered volumes of instant 'illustrated local history' washing ashore, each priced to move and sourced to nothing.
Gusty confidence with a 100% chance of 'they don't teach you this in school' captions. Corrections, if any, arrive after the wind has died.
Newsletter runoff carrying 'lost photos of your hometown' subject lines. Open rates elevated; verification rates flat.

specimen.status: Common and spreading; nocturnal in the algorithm, diurnal in the group chat.
specimen.habitat: History-nostalgia pages, 'the way things used to be' feeds, and any comment section that has recently used the word 'simpler.'
specimen.field_marks: Uniform sepia wash, impossibly crisp faces, hardware and clothing from three separate decades, and background signage that reads as fluent gibberish. Often captioned with a suspiciously precise year and a suspiciously round emotion.
specimen.call: Rare colorized photo, 1908 — they don't want you to see how life really was. Share before it's taken down.
specimen.confusion_species: Genuinely digitized archival photographs, which carry a collecting institution, a catalog number, and a boring provenance. If the only citation is a heart emoji, it is not from the archive.

spotter_tip: Count the buttons, then count the fingers, then look for a shadow that agrees with the sun. Real history is badly lit; slop is lit like a perfume ad.
tomorrow: Fabricated-heritage front weakens over the Basin, but a synthetic-recipe cell is developing over the Pinterest Shallows — expect casseroles that cannot physically set.

confirmed_sighting.report: A peer-reviewed study cataloging AI-generated 'slop' among online biomedical science education videos found the low-quality synthetic material widespread enough to pose real hazards to both learners and instructors relying on it. The Service notes that a confident narrator is not a citation.
